title: RAMI: Radiomics for LGE Assessment of Myocardial Infarction and Microvascular Obstruction
author: Santinha, João
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Late gadolinium Enhancement (LGE) is the method of choice for assessing myocardial infarction (MI) and viability, which can be used to guide revascularization decisions. LGE can also predict functional recovery revascularization. Moreover, dark no-reflow regions (or microvascular obstruction) within the LGE-enhanced areas, where blood flow remains inadequate post-revascularization, have been associated with worse clinical outcomes. Automatic segmentation and classification methods mainly focus on regions of LGE uptake and do not address areas of no-reflow. Radiomics has become popular for automatic quantitative feature extraction, which can potentially help identify diseased tissues. Here, we propose two radiomics models based on LGE features to improve MI diagnosis and detect no-reflow.
steps_raw: Late gadolinium Enhancement (LGE) is the method of choice for assessing myocardial infarction (MI) and viability, which can be used to guide revascularization decisions. LGE can also predict functional recovery revascularization. Moreover, dark no-reflow regions (or microvascular obstruction) within the LGE-enhanced areas, where blood flow remains inadequate post-revascularization, have been associated with worse clinical outcomes. Automatic segmentation and classification methods mainly focus on regions of LGE uptake and do not address areas of no-reflow. Radiomics has become popular for automatic quantitative feature extraction, which can potentially help identify diseased tissues. Here, we propose two radiomics models based on LGE features to improve MI diagnosis and detect no-reflow.
